title: Review: replace vaultrs with raw HTTP, fix JSON navigation bugs
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — The core JSON navigation code is well-tested and correct for normal Vault usage. The main risks are: (1) the byte-level skip path is less strict than the char-level path (undocumented asymmetry that will bite when the two paths diverge further); (2) GCP SM has documented unfixed zeroization gaps; (3) the vault addr= SSRF vector is low-severity now but could become high-severity if URI construction ever touches untrusted input.
Confidence: 10% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: GCP SM and Azure KV backends not integration-tested (can't verify JSON nav works on live responses); skip_string_b laxity could interact with a backend I haven't analyzed. Would raise confidence if: GCP SM integration test run and passing; audit of all backends that call extract_path/extract_path_field to confirm inputs are always from trusted servers.
